mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and business investment, which compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 55% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production growth exceeding 2.0 percent year-over-year, supply chain efficiency gains translate directly into higher corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth above 2.0 percent consistently coincides with flat or negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 2.5 percent, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to pressure highly valued growth stocks, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of observations where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth accelerates corporate revenue expansion, which directly lifts equity earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to exhibit a positive rank correlation with S&P 500 forward 60-day returns exceeding 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrently rising employment levels and stable unemployment rates indicate a healthy labor market that supports wage growth without triggering inflationary pressures, fostering positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and stable UNRATE does not outperform a random baseline in predicting positive 20-day forward returns over a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth decelerates below 1.0 percent month-over-month, it signals capacity constraints and weakening demand, which compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration below 1.0 percent month-over-month fails to predict negative S&P 500 forward returns in more than 40% of instances over a 3-year test period.
